page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9i.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 6982P-C physical cores running at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

This instance does not include local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for operating system and data needs. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The CPU allocation is fully dedicated with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, ensuring that workloads do not share execution resources with other tenants on the host.

Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated execution threads, the ecs.c9i.2xlarge offers a resource-dense profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. This hardware configuration is suitable for workloads such as web serving, batch processing, and compute-intensive applications that benefit from dedicated physical cores and a 3.2 GHz clock speed.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c9i.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 6982P-C physical cores running at 3.2 GHz. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it provides 16.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance does not include local storage, relying entirely on external network-attached storage, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. With a dedicated CPU allocation and a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, it avoids resource contention. This hardware profile is designed for compute-intensive workloads such as batch processing, web serving, and application hosting where dedicated processing power is prioritized over large memory allocations.
